name: Acme Design System
description: A confident enterprise Design System for clear product and sales communication.

colors.name: Acme Blue
colors.description: Use for primary actions, key headings, and moments that need strong Acme recognition.

colors.name: Deep Acme Blue
colors.description: Use for high-contrast backgrounds and serious executive sections.

colors.name: Signal Amber
colors.description: Use sparingly to highlight important metrics, calls to action, or alerts.

colors.name: Ink
colors.description: Use for primary body text and dense information that must remain highly legible.

colors.name: Cloud
colors.description: Use for subtle panels, page backgrounds, and low-emphasis separation.

colors.name: Growth Green
colors.description: Use for positive performance, confirmed states, and growth-oriented signals.

logo.name: Primary Logo
logo.description: Use as the standard logo on light slide backgrounds.

brandKit.designPrompt: Generate HTML and CSS that feels unmistakably Acme: confident, enterprise-ready, precise, and highly legible. Use a clean modern layout system with generous whitespace, disciplined alignment, and clear visual hierarchy. Prefer strong rectangular geometry, structured panels, subtle dividing rules, and data-friendly compositions over playful or decorative treatments. Build every screen or slide from semantic HTML with accessible landmarks, meaningful headings, and CSS custom properties that map directly to the Brand Kit color tokens. Use Acme Blue (#1B3A8F) for primary navigation, hero accents, key headings, and decisive calls to action; use Deep Acme Blue (#10245C) for executive, high-contrast, or premium sections; use Signal Amber (#F5A623) only as a sparing highlight for important metrics, alerts, or calls to action; use Ink (#111827) for primary text; use Cloud (#F3F4F6) for quiet surfaces and subtle panels; use Growth Green (#2E7D32) only for confirmed success, positive movement, or growth signals. Keep backgrounds mostly white or Cloud with restrained blue blocks. Typography should be crisp, professional, and easy to scan: large confident titles, medium-weight section headers, compact supporting copy, and tabular numerals for metrics. Favor CSS grid and flexbox, consistent spacing increments, rounded corners only when subtle, and soft shadows only when they clarify hierarchy. Avoid gradients unless extremely subtle, avoid neon colors, avoid whimsical illustration styles, avoid excessive animation, and never let amber dominate the composition. Logo placement should be clear and uncluttered on light backgrounds. The final HTML/CSS should look like a polished enterprise SaaS presentation or product marketing page: authoritative, calm, measurable, and trustworthy.

assets.name: Primary Logo SVG
assets.description: Copy this logo asset next to generated HTML and reference it with a local relative path.

persistentElements.name: Brand Logo
persistentElements.description: Required recurring logo placement for every slide.

persistentElements.name: Slide Number
persistentElements.description: Required recurring slide number placement for every slide.

persistentElements.name: Accent Corner
persistentElements.description: Optional governed accent shape for slides that need additional brand energy.
